steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states researchers ‘did not observe even one’ of a species — meaning the blank must name a species and time period where the graph shows zero individuals. The lined rockskipper (dashed line with squares) reads zero for all four months: January, April, July, and October 2001. The barred flagtail shows 15, 10, 8, and 16 across those months; the combtooth blenny shows 62, 3, 3, and 2.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'barred flagtail in October of 2001': The barred flagtail had 16 individuals in October — not zero. ✗
  • Choice B — 'combtooth blenny in January of 2001': The combtooth blenny had 62 individuals in January — its highest count. ✗
  • Choice C — 'barred flagtail in January of 2001': The barred flagtail had 15 individuals in January — not zero. ✗
  • Choice D — 'lined rockskipper in April and July of 2001': The lined rockskipper shows zero individuals in both April and July, matching the claim that researchers did not observe even one. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
